keys.description: Format of transaction_number. {year} = 4-digit calendar year (from effective_date); {seq:08d} = zero-padded 8-digit sequence per tenant per year. The Postgres advisory lock keyed on (tenant_id, year) guarantees monotonicity.

true = sequence resets to 1 on Jan 1 of each calendar year (default). false = sequence never resets (single monotonic sequence forever). Regulator-preferred: true (per-year restart makes financial-year audits easier).
Postgres advisory-lock acquire timeout for the transaction-number generator. Bounded to prevent lock contention from spiking under high concurrent record rates.

Chart-of-accounts entries enabled per default. Tenants can override the enabled list via the transaction settings (a tenant that never issues chargebacks can drop chargebacks_expense — but the 14 above are baked into the module's Recorder + Reporter code paths).

Per-account high-watermark thresholds (cents) for AccountBalanceThresholdCrossed alerts. accounts_receivable > $50k = collection risk; refunds_owed > $10k = needs disbursement; tax_payable > $5k = filing due.
Per-account low-watermark thresholds (cents). cash < $1k = liquidity risk. Tenant admins customize via settings.
Hour of day (UTC) for nightly reconciler. Defaults to 02:00 — off-peak, before the monthly report window.
Baseline for reconciler alerts. ANY imbalance above 0 is a P0 signal.
Chunking bound for the reconciler. Larger tenants split into multiple runs.

true (default) = set tenant.transaction_halt_new = true when LedgerImbalanceDetected fires + refuse new transactions until resolved. false = alert but keep accepting new transactions. Regulator-preferred: true.
Where transactionobserver reads the tenant's base currency from. Cross-currency transactions compute base_amount_cents against this currency.

Fallback when tenant.base_currency is null (should never happen — tenant provisioning always sets it). Regulator-preferred: fail hard rather than silently default; the module's observer errors when the tenant.base_currency is missing rather than using this fallback in production.
Default rate provider. Enum: openexchangerates / fixer_io / ecb_daily / manual. Tenant admins can override via settings.

How long an ExchangeRateResolver caches a fetched rate. 1h default. Regulators want the rate at commit-time frozen; caching within an hour is acceptable for consistency across a batch of transactions.

Maximum age of a cached rate before the resolver forces a fresh fetch. 6h. If the provider is down for > 6h, the resolver falls back to the ecb_daily official rate; if THAT fails, the transaction is refused (returns TRANSACTION_EXCHANGE_RATE_UNAVAILABLE).
Default retention window. Enterprise tenants with transaction_extended_retention entitlement: 10 years.
true (default) = every purge is preceded by an atomic export to compliance-storage. false = hard-purge without archive (DANGEROUS — NEVER flip off in production; SOX 802 requires the archive).
true (default) = every manual reversal via POST /transactions/{id}/reverse requires a written note ≥ 10 chars. Regulator-preferred: true.
Minimum required amendment_note length. Prevents blank / one-word notes that don't survive regulator review.

The reversal_reason values accepted by the module. Manual reversals via HTTP always use 'accounting_adjustment'; the other three are auto-recorded by RecordRefundIssuedTransactionJob / RecordChargebackTransactionJob.
